name: Affordable Debt Consolidation - Plano Texas
description_short: Texas-based debt settlement firm specializing in high-income earners with $50K-$300K+ in unsecured debt, offering negotiated payoffs through FDIC-insured accounts.
description_long:
Affordable Debt Consolidation operates as a debt settlement company focused exclusively on Texas residents, particularly those with higher incomes and substantial debt loads. The company positions itself as a specialist for individuals carrying $50,000 to $300,000+ in credit card and personal loan debt, distinguishing itself from national debt relief providers by claiming local expertise and state-specific knowledge.

The company's primary service is debt settlement (also called debt negotiation or debt resolution), where they negotiate directly with creditors to reduce total amounts owed. Rather than consolidating debts into a new loan, the model requires enrolling debts and depositing monthly payments into FDIC-insured accounts, which are then used to pay negotiated settlements. They also reference debt counseling, consolidation loan referrals through affiliate partners, and bankruptcy law firm referrals as secondary options.

Affordable Debt Consolidation distinguishes itself through performance-based fees (15% of enrolled debt, claimed to be up to 40% lower than industry standard rates of 25%), a free consultation model with no upfront obligation, and A+ BBB rating with zero complaints reported. The company emphasizes transparent communication, explicitly stating they cannot and do not guarantee specific settlement percentages due to federal and Texas consumer protection laws, positioning themselves against competitors they characterize as using "bait-and-switch" tactics.

A critical caveat: debt settlement significantly impacts credit scores during the enrollment period, as debts are typically not paid in full until settlement. The company's model assumes borrowers have disposable income to fund settlement accounts while accounts age unpaid. Success depends heavily on creditor willingness to negotiate, which varies. Their claims of lower fees and industry advantages should be verified independently, as should their BBB rating and complaint history.

pricing.guarantee_details: Contact provider for current pricing and guarantee details.

pros: Performance-based fee structure (15% of enrolled debt) with no upfront charges until debts are settled
Claims fees are up to 40% lower than national competitors charging 25% industry standard rates
A+ BBB rating with zero complaints reported on their profile
Free, no-obligation initial consultation with Texas debt specialists
FDIC-insured dedicated accounts for settlement funds
Explicitly transparent about legal limitations—refuses to guarantee specific settlement percentages
Phone-based access to Texas debt specialists (469-663-6200)
cons: Debt settlement typically requires 3-5+ years to complete and significantly damages credit scores during enrollment
No guarantee of settlement percentages or timeline to debt freedom; outcomes depend on creditor cooperation
Limited service scope—primarily debt settlement; other options (counseling, loans, bankruptcy) appear to be referrals or affiliate arrangements, not direct services
Website lacks clear disclosure of success rates, average settlement percentages, or typical program duration for enrolled clients
Debt settlement can trigger creditor lawsuits if accounts remain unpaid during negotiation process
best_for: Higher-income Texas earners with $50K-$300K+ in unsecured credit card and personal loan debt
Individuals who have disposable income to fund monthly settlement accounts but are overwhelmed by multiple creditors
Borrowers with damaged credit already and no immediate need for new credit who want to resolve debt faster than minimum payments allow
Texas residents seeking local debt expertise and who prioritize transparent, non-guaranteed communication over inflated promises
diagnosis: Best for Texas-based, higher-income borrowers with $50K-$300K+ in unsecured debt who have cash flow to fund settlement accounts and can tolerate significant credit score damage over 3-5+ years. Critical caveat: debt settlement is not guaranteed, outcomes vary by creditor, and the credit impact may disqualify borrowers from future borrowing during the program—this is suitable only for those committed to debt resolution over rapid credit recovery.
services: Debt settlement negotiation with creditors (primary service)
Monthly budgeting and payment management aligned with settlement strategy
Per-debt negotiation and settlement (one account at a time approach)
Creditor communication and negotiation on client behalf
Debt counseling referral services (non-bankruptcy credit counseling)
Debt consolidation loan referrals through affiliate lender platform
Bankruptcy law firm referrals (non-bankruptcy alternative exploration)
Performance-based fee structure (15% of enrolled debt, charged upon settlement)
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
address: 101 E Park Blvd Suite 600, Plano, TX 75074
